author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: 100 stars is inclusive and add quality-check workflow

Two related follow-ups to PR #3306:

1. **Star rule is inclusive**: change "More than 100 stargazers" to
   "At least 100 stargazers" in CONTRIBUTING.md and the PR template.
   Repos with exactly 100 stars qualify — the bar is 100 flat, not 101.
   Hidden-gem range narrowed to 75-99 accordingly.

2. **Add the PR quality-check workflow** that was drafted in #3307 but
   did not land in master. Runs on every PR touching README.md,
   extracts new github.com URLs, and checks each one against the
   automatable hard rules (stars, contributors, archived, license,
   with stale-commit shown as a soft warning).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: restore 2 repos with exactly 100 stars

After the star rule was relaxed from >100 to >=100, two repos that
were previously queued for removal now pass: PredicateFlow (100★, 7
contribs) and Ecno (100★, 4 contribs). Restored.

GraphLayout (100★, 1 contrib) still fails the contributor rule and
stays removed.

Net PR 3308 delta: 497 removals (was 499).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
Merge pull request #3306 from vsouza/chore/tighten-inclusion-rules

chore: raise inclusion bar with stricter contribution rules

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: remove entries failing the new star/contributor rules

Removes 499 entries (491 unique repos across 58 sections) that fail at
least one of the new hard rules introduced in #3306:

- 391 repos have <= 100 stars
- 215 repos have <= 1 non-bot contributor
- 115 fail both rules

Stale-commit (>24 months since last push) is a soft signal in the new
rules and is intentionally NOT used as a removal criterion here.
Truly abandoned projects will be pruned through targeted follow-up
audits.

Every repo was verified against the GitHub REST API as part of the
2,030-repo scan. Paid-product auditing (another new hard rule) is
manual and will be handled in a follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: move stale-commit from hard rule to soft signal

After auditing the existing list against the original draft of rules, we
found that a hard ">24 months since last commit" rule would disqualify
~1,500 repos — many of which are stable, widely-used "complete"
libraries (Masonry, PureLayout, MagicalRecord, GPU Image). Inactivity
alone is a poor proxy for quality.

Moves "commit in the last 24 months" from the hard rules to the soft
signals in both CONTRIBUTING.md and the PR template, and adjusts
deletion criteria accordingly. Prolonged inactivity can still be a
reason for removal through periodic audits or community-proposed
issues/PRs, but is no longer an automatic trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: raise inclusion bar with stricter contribution rules

Rewrites CONTRIBUTING.md and PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md to enforce a
clearer, stricter bar for inclusion in the list.

New hard rules (all required):
- More than 100 stars (was 50)
- Commit in last 24 months (was "recent", now explicit)
- More than one contributor (was "can be rejected", now required)
- Not archived
- OSI-approved open-source license
- Supports Swift Package Manager
- Targets iOS 14+ / tvOS 14+ / macOS 11+ / watchOS 7+
- Built with Swift 5.5+ (or Obj-C on Xcode 15+)
- English README with install + usage example
- Not a paid library, paid app, or paid course (free tier counts)

Changes from previous rules:
- Tests dropped from hard rule to soft signal
- Carthage no longer required; SPM is now the baseline
- iOS/Swift floors bumped to iOS 14 / Swift 5.5
- Added "hidden gem" exception for 75-100 star projects with justification
- Added explicit deletion criteria
- Added soft signals borrowed from awesome-go / awesome-python
  (SemVer tag, repo age, promotional language, category minimum)

PR template becomes an actionable checklist with an explicit warning
that unchecked boxes mean the PR will be closed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: remove archived and 404 repos

Removes 275 entries across 48 sections where the referenced GitHub repo
is archived (234) or returns 404 / 451 (34). All removals were verified
programmatically via the GitHub REST API.

No entries were added, reordered, or reworded. Diff is 0 insertions,
276 deletions (1 extra for a continuation line on a multi-line entry).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: alphabetize entries within each section

author: Vinicius Souza
message:
chore: alphabetize entries within each section

Sorts list entries alphabetically by link text (case-insensitive) within
every section of the README. The `## Contents` TOC is left untouched
since its order must match the section sequence.

Also normalizes 3 malformed entries that had a double-space after the
dash (`-  [Name]` → `- [Name]`).

No entries added, removed, or reworded. 136 groups sorted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

No entries were added, removed, reordered, or reworded.
